title: Best Wireless Dog Fence & Containment (No Digging Required)
meta: Wireless and semi-wireless fences that keep your dog in the yard without burying wire.
intro: A wireless fence uses a central transmitter to define a circular boundary. It's fast to set up but less precise than buried wire.

sections.body: Wireless units make a circle around the transmitter; odd-shaped yards need wired systems.
Static, vibration and tone modes exist; train gradually, never as punishment.
The collar needs charging; check range vs. your yard.

faq.a: Dense foliage can weaken the signal; test before relying on it.
Yes when used with proper training; follow the manual's intro steps.
